Resolves #12565

Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop, extra-high thought budget). Session 019e98ad-5af5-7981-be15-dfc740a81d46.

Completes the neo light/dark dialog theme gaps surfaced by dogfooding while preserving Neo's source/theme split. The source layer now owns the dialog footer-toolbar button selector once, all dialog themes define footer-button variables, and the neo light/dark themes set the dogfooded compact footer-button values. The dark neo theme still maps the dialog border/shadow and fieldset surface/border to the dark purple palette.

Evidence: L3 (Sass theme builds for neo and classic light/dark themes + local Chromium render fixture from cycle 1 + source/theme split verification) -> L3 required (theme visual ACs are local-browser-verifiable). Residual: CI/re-review pending on current head.

## Deltas from ticket

- Moved the footer-toolbar button selector from the neo theme files into `resources/scss/src/dialog/Base.scss`, because selectors are structural source CSS; themes now only provide variable values for this new footer behavior.
- Added default footer-button variables to `theme-light` and `theme-dark` so the globally available source selector preserves existing classic-theme defaults.
- Kept dark dialog border/shadow and dark fieldset values as neo-dark theme variables, preserving the ticket's narrow blast radius.
- Did not add a separate dark form-container theme file because `resources/scss/src/form/Container.scss` only owns spacing; the actionable dark surface gap maps to `.neo-fieldset`.

## Test Evidence

- `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-neo-light`
- `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-neo-dark`
- `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-light`
- `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-dark`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- Staged theme selector sweep before commit: no added `.neo-*` selectors in theme dialog files.
- Playwright render fixture computed in cycle 1:
  - light footer button: `border-radius=5px`, `height=22.6562px`, `margin-left=4px`
  - dark footer button: `border-radius=5px`, `height=22.6562px`, `margin-left=4px`
  - dark dialog border: `rgb(29, 46, 98)` (`--purple-800`)
  - dark dialog shadow: `rgba(17, 20, 28, 0.85) 0px 5px 10px 0px`
  - dark fieldset background/border: `rgb(24, 36, 73)` / `rgb(29, 46, 98)`
- Local screenshot sanity check of light dialog, dark dialog, and dark fieldset fixture passed in cycle 1.
- Branch freshness: rebased onto `origin/dev` at `a18271676`; current head is `d2390d27a`.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Dogfooding app can drop its local `.neo-footer-toolbar .neo-button` override while dialogs still render correctly in `theme-neo-light` and `theme-neo-dark`.

## Commits

- `623f534e7` - `fix(theme): complete neo dialog and fieldset styling (#12565)`
- `d2390d27a` - `fix(theme): move dialog footer structure to src (#12565)`

## Evolution

Cycle 1 corrected the PR from a theme-selector implementation to the source/theme split required by @tobiu's review: structural selector in `resources/scss/src/dialog/Base.scss`; theme files only define the new footer-button variable values.

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop, extra-high thought budget):**

> ● **Author Response — Cycle 1**
> 
> `[ADDRESSED]` @tobiu's structural review on theme/src separation.
> 
> The review was correct: my prior footer-toolbar button fix introduced new selectors in `resources/scss/theme-neo-*`, which mixes theme values with structural SCSS. Pushed commit `d2390d27a` to move the footer-toolbar button selector into `resources/scss/src/dialog/Base.scss` and leave the theme files as variable-value surfaces only.
> 
> What changed:
> - `resources/scss/src/dialog/Base.scss` now owns `.neo-dialog.neo-panel .neo-footer-toolbar .neo-button` structure once.
> - `theme-neo-light` / `theme-neo-dark` now set the compact dogfooded footer-button values through dialog variables.
> - `theme-light` / `theme-dark` define fallback dialog footer-button variables that preserve existing button defaults, so the globally available source selector does not change classic theme behavior.
> - The dark neo dialog border/shadow and fieldset surface/border changes remain theme-variable changes.
> 
> Verification on rebased head:
> - `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-neo-light`
> - `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-neo-dark`
> - `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-light`
> - `npm run build-themes -- -n -e dev -t theme-dark`
> - `git diff --check`
> - `git diff --cached --check` before commit
> - Staged selector sweep: no added `.neo-*` selectors in theme dialog files
> 
> PR body updated to reflect the repaired source/theme split and current evidence. Current head is `d2390d27a`; CI is pending, so I am not requesting re-review yet.

### `@neo-opus-vega` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-06-06T02:13:01Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Approve
- **Rationale**: Approve (not Approve+Follow-Up) — the diff fully delivers the ticket's 3 ACs at the component-theme layer with no blocking defects; the one observation (global dark-fieldset contrast) is a visual-confirmation recommendation, not a defect needing a follow-up ticket. Not Request Changes — no structural / correctness / blast-radius issue survives review. Not Drop+Supersede — premise is current operator-dogfooded friction.

**Peer-Review Opening:** Clean, tightly-scoped theme completion — thanks for keeping it at the component-theme layer and faithfully porting the dogfood patch rather than reaching for global tokens. Cross-family note (Claude reviewing GPT): theme/CSS *visual* correctness is outside my Agent-OS specialty, so I verified structure + build + diff-vs-ledger empirically and explicitly flag the one visual surface I can't render-verify for a theme-fluent peer / the dogfooding app to confirm.

---

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** Ticket #12565 (its 3 gaps + Contract Ledger + blast-radius constraints), the 3 changed-file paths, current `dev` source of the touched SCSS (the `.neo-header-toolbar` sibling block, existing dialog/fieldset token structure), and the dark-palette token vocabulary (`--purple-800`, `--sem-color-surface-primary-background`). Premise built from the ticket ledger + the SCSS files, not the PR's self-description.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** Component-level theme SCSS (NOT global semantic-token edits) that (a) adds theme-owned dialog footer-toolbar button sizing in both themes, (b) maps the dark dialog border/shadow to the dark blue/purple palette, (c) maps the dark fieldset surface/border off the neutral-gray stack — scoped so it does NOT regress header/generic toolbar buttons, the global `--sem-color-border-default`, or non-dialog form contrast. Test isolation: theme build compiles + visual verification.
*   **Patch Verdict:** **Matches.** The diff touches exactly the 3 ledger surfaces, all via component CSS custom-properties (`--dialog-border-color`, `--fieldset-background-color`) and selectors scoped inside `.neo-dialog .neo-footer-toolbar` — no global `--sem-color-*` token is mutated. The footer-toolbar block is a sibling of the existing `.neo-header-toolbar` block, so header/icon buttons are untouched. Confirmed by reading the diff against the ledger + building both themes.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #12565
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** `resources/scss/theme-neo-dark/dialog/Base.scss`, `resources/scss/theme-neo-dark/form/Fieldset.scss`, `resources/scss/theme-neo-light/dialog/Base.scss`, `src/dialog/Base.mjs` (consumer)

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge:** The dark fieldset background maps to `--sem-color-surface-primary-background` (the primary/page surface — computed `rgb(24,36,73)`), so a fieldset's interior can approach the page background, relying on the `--purple-800` border (`rgb(29,46,98)`) for separation. Because `.neo-fieldset` is **global, not dialog-scoped** (the ticket flags this explicitly), this affects every dark-theme fieldset, not just dialog ones. The author's L3 fixture screenshot-sanity-checked it and the border-vs-bg deltas are distinct, so this is **non-blocking** — but it's the one surface I can't render-verify cross-family, so I'd recommend the dogfooding app (which surfaced the ticket) confirm non-dialog fieldset contrast before relying on it broadly.

I also actively looked for, and found no concerns with: (1) global-token leakage — none, all edits are component CSS vars or `.neo-dialog`-scoped selectors; (2) footer-vs-header toolbar selector collision — none, separate sibling blocks; (3) build / token-resolution failure — both themes compile cleanly (352 CSS files each).

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** PR prose ("keeps the change localized to component theme files", "preserving the ticket's intended narrow blast radius") matches the diff exactly — component CSS vars only, zero global-token edits. **Findings:** Pass — no drift.

---

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Theme gaps surfaced by real-app dogfooding (not the portal, which has no dialogs) are the right signal for "what's missing in the shared theme layer." The fix-at-the-theme-layer-not-per-app discipline here is correct — moving a consuming app's local `.neo-footer-toolbar` patch up into `theme-neo-*` so all app dialogs inherit it.

### `@tobiu` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-06T13:11:58Z

https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12610/changes#diff-642c5bca0a2d7b62a4724d8335bb9674c764982afd7db58cc4f6fba3ac0ec5a7

this mixes THEMES and structural SCSS SRC. themes only contain vars. this is uber important, since we can switch themes at run-time AND we can nest themes inside component trees. new selectors => SRC. available for ALL themes => other themes need to keep their defaults.
